title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify in tutorials page
description: The tutorials page uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: renderedHtml }} where renderedHtml is produced by a custom markdown-to-HTML parser (renderMarkdown function) that processes fetched tutorial content. No DOMPurify sanitization is applied before rendering. If tutorial content is user-editable or sourced from an untrusted API, this is a stored XSS vector. The custom parser converts markdown to HTML including , , and other tags, allowing injection of malicious attributes.
fix: Import DOMPurify and wrap renderedHtml: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: DOMPurify.sanitize(renderedHtml, { ALLOWED_TAGS: [...], ALLOWED_ATTR: [...] }) }}. Add DOMPurify import at top of file.

title: Custom markdown parser creates unsanitized tags from user content
description: The renderMarkdown() function at line 311 uses a regex replacement to convert markdown image syntax to tags: html.replace(/!\[([^\]]*)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, '
fix: Either sanitize the final HTML with DOMPurify (preferred) or validate URLs in the regex replacement to only allow https:// and relative paths. Add: if (!url.startsWith('https://') && !url.startsWith('/')) return '';

title: Custom markdown parser creates unsanitized <a> tags with target=_blank
description: The renderMarkdown() function creates <a href="$2"> tags from markdown links without URL validation. While rel="noopener noreferrer" is added, the href itself is not validated, allowing javascript: protocol injection. This is mitigated by the admin-only context but is still a stored XSS risk if tutorial content is sourced from external files or user input.
fix: Validate href values or use DOMPurify sanitization on the final output. DOMPurify automatically strips javascript: URLs.

title: new Function() used for syntax validation - audited and sandboxed
description: new Function(code) is used at line 112 for syntax validation only (parsing, not execution). The actual code execution happens in a vm.Context sandbox with strict timeout (5s), frozen context, and blocked API access. The code is additionally screened by BLOCKED_PATTERNS that prevent Function() usage in user code. Marked as VERIFIED SAFE by security audit 2026-03-15. Noting for completeness but this is properly mitigated.
fix: No action required. Already properly sandboxed with multiple layers of protection. Consider adding a comment explaining why eslint disable is used.

title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage summary - 12 of 14 properly sanitized
description: Audit of all 14 files using dangerouslySetInnerHTML: SAFE (DOMPurify): checkout/page.tsx, blog/[slug]/page.tsx, cours/[slug]/page.tsx, CampaignEditor.tsx, EmailComposer.tsx, ConversationThread.tsx (x2), comptabilite/recherche/page.tsx. SAFE (JSON-LD with < escape): JsonLd.tsx, Breadcrumbs.tsx, category/[slug]/page.tsx. SAFE (hardcoded snippet, no user input): layout.tsx anti-FOUC script. SAFE (DOMPurify on content): learn/[slug]/page.tsx. UNSAFE: tutoriels/page.tsx (no sanitization - see C1-SEC-S-151).
fix: Fix tutoriels/page.tsx (C1-SEC-S-151). All other usages are properly secured.

title: 106 pages lack any form of i18n (useI18n, useTranslation, or server i18n)
description: 106 of 354 page.tsx files do not import or use any i18n system (neither useI18n/useTranslation client hooks nor getServerLocale/createServerTranslator server functions). Many of these are thin server wrappers delegating to client components that DO use i18n, but at least 60+ pages contain hardcoded strings. Breakdown: 60 admin pages, 18 public pages, ~16 platform/telephonie/CRM pages. The project's CLAUDE.md requires ALL user-facing text to use t().
fix: For each page: (1) If it's a thin wrapper delegating to a client component with i18n, no action needed. (2) If it contains hardcoded strings, add useI18n() hook and replace strings with t() calls. Add missing keys to all 22 locale files. Priority: public pages first (user-facing), then admin pages.

title: Super-admin platform dashboard entirely hardcoded French
description: The platform dashboard has zero i18n. All strings are hardcoded French: 'Plateforme Koraline', 'Nouveau Client', 'Tenants', 'Utilisateurs', 'Produits', 'Commandes', status labels, form labels. This page doesn't even import useI18n or useTranslation.
fix: Import useI18n from '@/i18n/client'. Replace all hardcoded strings with t() calls. Add keys to all 22 locale JSON files under an 'admin.platform' namespace.

title: Assisted setup wizard entirely hardcoded French
description: The tenant setup wizard for Attitudes VIP employees has zero i18n. All labels, step titles, form fields, button texts, and validation messages are hardcoded French: 'Informations client', 'Nom de l'entreprise', 'Courriel du proprietaire', 'Selection du plan', 'Continuer', etc. No i18n hooks imported.
fix: Add useI18n() hook and replace all hardcoded strings. Add translation keys to all 22 locale files.

title: Public onboarding page entirely hardcoded French - customer-facing
description: The post-payment onboarding wizard that tenants see after subscribing is fully hardcoded in French. This is customer-facing and will be seen by non-French speakers. All 5 steps (password, branding, industry, first product, recap) use French strings. Industry labels like 'E-commerce generique', 'Sante / Supplements' are not translatable. No i18n hooks imported.
fix: This is customer-facing and CRITICAL for i18n. Import useI18n, add t() calls, and add all keys to 22 locale files. The INDUSTRIES array labels should use t() calls.

title: 18 customer-facing public pages lack i18n
description: 18 pages under src/app/(public)/ have no i18n integration. These are publicly accessible and customer-facing. Includes: onboarding, signup, solutions (5 pages), docs, support, resources/webinaires, presse, carrieres, privacy, terms, plan-du-site, checkout/[slug], cours/[slug], order tracking. Some may be server wrappers delegating to i18n-aware client components, but most contain hardcoded French text.
fix: Audit each page. For those with hardcoded strings, add i18n support. Priority order: onboarding > signup > solutions > checkout > others.

title: Hardcoded placeholder strings not using t() in admin forms
description: Approximately 183 input placeholder attributes use hardcoded strings (e.g., placeholder="Search...", placeholder="Nom du projet") instead of t() calls. Found in 49+ files, predominantly in admin/comptabilite/, admin/telephonie/, admin/crm/, and admin/platform/ sections. This violates the project's i18n rule that ALL user-facing text must use t().
fix: Replace each hardcoded placeholder with placeholder={t('namespace.placeholderKey')}. Add corresponding keys to all 22 locale files.

title: Entire telephonie section (16 pages) lacks i18n
description: All 16 pages in the admin/telephonie/ section have no i18n integration. These are wrapper pages delegating to *Client.tsx components, but the client components (ParametresClient, GroupesClient, CampagnesClient, etc.) also use hardcoded French strings. This is the largest section without i18n coverage.
fix: Add i18n to all telephonie client components. Create admin.telephonie.* translation keys in all 22 locale files.

title: Platform management and subscription pages lack i18n
description: The platform management pages (platform/page.tsx, platform/tenants/[id], platform/assisted-setup) and subscription management pages (abonnement/plan, abonnement/domaine, abonnement/modules, abonnement/page) all use hardcoded French strings without i18n. These are used by super-admins and tenants managing their subscriptions.
fix: Add useI18n() to each page and replace hardcoded strings with t() calls.

title: Several CRM sub-pages lack i18n (forecast, adherence, qa, workflow-analytics, etc.)
description: At least 10 CRM pages lack i18n: forecast, adherence, qa, workflow-analytics, activity-reports, attribution, call-center-kpis, call-analytics, churn, clv. These are wrapper pages delegating to client components. The client components themselves use a mix of i18n and hardcoded strings.
fix: Add i18n to client components that still have hardcoded strings.

title: Zero <caption> elements on any table in the codebase (130+ tables)
description: The codebase has 191 <table> elements across 130 files, but zero <caption> elements. WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 requires data tables to have accessible names. Screen readers use <caption> to announce what a table contains. Without it, users navigating by table landmarks hear 'table' with no context. This affects all admin data tables, accounting reports, CRM lists, and shop comparison tables.
fix: Add <caption className="sr-only">{t('table.caption.key')}</caption> as the first child of each <table>. Alternatively, use aria-label or aria-labelledby on the table element. Priority: public-facing tables first (shop, account), then admin tables.

title: 38 interactive <div> elements with onClick but missing keyboard support
description: Found 38 <div onClick=...> occurrences across 25 files. Non-semantic elements with click handlers need role='button', tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown/onKeyPress handlers for keyboard accessibility. Files include: Softphone.tsx, media/videos, media/library, media/images, comptabilite/rsde, comptabilite/immobilisations, comptabilite/ocr, comptabilite/recurrentes, crm/quotes, and more. Only 2 files in the entire codebase use role='button' (duplicates and emails pages).
fix: For each <div onClick>: (1) Replace with <button> if it performs an action (preferred). (2) If <div> must remain, add: role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') handler() }}. The same fix applies to 8 <tr>/<td>/<li> elements with onClick.

title: Most form labels not programmatically linked to inputs (htmlFor missing)
description: The codebase has <label> elements in 168 files (893 occurrences) but htmlFor= in only 34 files (165 occurrences). This means roughly 80% of labels are not programmatically linked to their inputs. Example: comptabilite/paie/page.tsx has 17 labels, zero with htmlFor. comptabilite/projets-couts has 36 labels, zero with htmlFor. Without htmlFor (or nesting), clicking the label does not focus the input, and screen readers may not announce the label when the input is focused. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 and SC 4.1.2.
fix: For each <label>, add htmlFor='id' and ensure the corresponding <input> has a matching id attribute. Alternative: nest the <input> inside the <label>. Use a codemod or regex-based script to automate: find all <label> without htmlFor, generate unique ids.

title: Minimal tabIndex usage across interactive elements
description: Only 13 files in the entire codebase use tabIndex, with a total of 16 occurrences. Given 736 files with onClick handlers and 38 non-button interactive elements, keyboard navigation coverage is very low. Custom interactive widgets (kanban boards, media galleries, inline editors, softphone controls) likely cannot be reached via Tab key navigation.
fix: Audit all custom interactive components. Add tabIndex={0} to focusable non-button interactive elements. For composite widgets (kanban, gallery), implement arrow key navigation pattern per WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices.

file: 4 files with native tags in non-email contexts
title: Native tags used instead of Next.js Image component (4 files)
description: 4 files use native HTML tags: (1) account/settings/page.tsx:705 - MFA QR code (data URL, acceptable), (2) TemplateBuilder.tsx:43 - email HTML template (acceptable), (3) tutoriels/page.tsx:311 - markdown parser output (acceptable in HTML string), (4) mobile/receipt-capture/page.tsx:88 - receipt preview (data URL, acceptable). All have alt attributes. The codebase generally uses next/image correctly (88 files import from 'next/image').
fix: No critical action needed. The 4 uses of native are justified (data URLs and HTML string generation). Continue using next/image for all other cases.

file: 96 files with alt= out of 88 using next/image
title: Image alt attributes present but quality varies
description: 96 files have alt= attributes, covering 137 occurrences. All next/image usages (88 files) include alt props, which is good. However, many alt texts are generic or non-descriptive: 'MFA QR Code', 'Logo', empty strings for decorative images. Some dynamic images (product images, user avatars, media library) may have empty or repetitive alt texts depending on database content. No systematic pattern for decorative images (alt='') vs informative images.
fix: Audit alt text quality. For product images, use product name as alt. For decorative images, use alt='' explicitly. For user avatars, use username. Add alt text guidelines to component documentation.

title: Skip-to-content navigation implemented correctly
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The SkipToContent component exists and is integrated into shop, public, and admin layouts. Translation keys exist in all 22 locale files. This is a WCAG 2.4.1 requirement that is properly implemented.
fix: No action needed. Verify the target id='main-content' exists on the main content area of each layout.

title: 130+ inline hardcoded color values may cause contrast issues
description: Found 130 instances of inline style={{ color: '#...' }} across 26 files. Concentrated in: legal pages (cookies-en.tsx:11, conditions:6, confidentialite:5), contact/page.tsx:27, a-propos/page.tsx:13, BlogComments.tsx:6, account/invoices:16. These hardcoded colors bypass the design system tokens (ios26-tokens.css) and may not maintain proper contrast ratios in both light and dark themes. The project uses Tailwind CSS with dark mode support, but inline styles don't respect dark mode.
fix: Replace inline color styles with Tailwind CSS classes that support dark mode: text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 instead of style={{ color: '#334155' }}. For brand colors, use CSS custom properties from ios26-tokens.css.

title: 62 public and shop pages missing SEO metadata
description: Only 42 of 354 pages export metadata or generateMetadata(). Among customer-facing pages: 23 of 42 public pages and 39 of 48 shop pages lack metadata. Missing metadata pages include: gift-cards, learn/[slug], portal, webinars, refund-policy, rewards, ambassador, track-order, checkout/success, subscriptions, search, videos, compare, community, bundles, change-password, shipping-policy, and all 18+ account/* pages. Each of these renders in search engines with generic Next.js defaults.
fix: Add export const metadata or generateMetadata() to each page. Priority: (1) public pages indexed by search engines (contact, aide, tarifs, a-propos, etc.), (2) shop pages that could appear in SERPs (gift-cards, bundles, webinars), (3) account pages (lower priority - authenticated).

title: Loading and error boundaries well-covered (339 loading, 126 error files)
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The codebase has excellent coverage of Next.js loading states (339 loading.tsx files for 354 pages) and error boundaries (126 error.tsx files). Additionally, 45 pages use inline loading patterns (isLoading, Skeleton, Suspense). There is also a global-error.tsx at the app root.
fix: No critical action needed. Consider adding error.tsx files for the remaining ~228 routes that don't have them (mostly admin pages that share parent error boundaries).

title: 113 pages may lack responsive design (no sm:/md:/lg:/xl: breakpoints)
description: 113 of 354 page.tsx files contain no responsive Tailwind breakpoint classes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:), flex-col, grid-cols, or hidden utility classes. Many are thin server component wrappers that delegate rendering to client components which DO have responsive design. However, at least 30-40 pages are actual client components with layout content but no responsive breakpoints. These pages may not adapt properly to mobile screens.
fix: Audit the 113 pages, filtering out thin wrappers. For pages with actual content: (1) Add responsive padding (px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8), (2) Use responsive grid (grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3), (3) Stack columns on mobile (flex-col sm:flex-row). Focus on platform/page.tsx (grid-cols-4 without sm: variant).

title: console.log in middleware - intentional structured logging
description: Found console.log(JSON.stringify(logEntry)) in middleware.ts at line 275. This is intentional structured JSON logging for request monitoring, not a debug statement. The other console.log occurrences in api-docs/page.tsx are inside code example strings, not actual console calls. No actual debug console.log statements found in production code.
fix: No action required. Consider using a structured logger (e.g., pino) instead of console.log for better log management in production.

title: 68% of pages are client components - many could be server components
description: 242 of 354 page.tsx files use 'use client' directive, while only 112 are server components. Many admin pages are large client components (20+ over 1000 lines) that could benefit from a server/client split pattern: server component for data fetching, client component for interactivity. The largest client component pages are 2425, 2328, 1986, 1835, 1815 lines. This increases JavaScript bundle size and prevents server-side rendering optimizations.
fix: For large client component pages (>500 lines): (1) Extract data fetching into a server component wrapper, (2) Pass data as props to a smaller client component, (3) Use React Server Components for static parts of the page. Priority: public pages first (SEO benefit), then frequently accessed admin pages.

title: Platform dashboard uses grid-cols-4 without responsive breakpoints
description: The platform dashboard stats grid uses className="grid grid-cols-4 gap-4" which forces 4 columns even on mobile screens. On a 375px screen, each card would be ~80px wide, making content unreadable. No sm:/md:/lg: breakpoint variants are used.
fix: Change to: className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4"

title: Many admin modals/drawers lack focus trap
description: Admin pages use custom modal implementations (useState-driven visibility toggles) that don't implement focus trapping. When a modal opens, users can Tab outside the modal to background elements. The ui/Modal.tsx component does have aria-modal and focus management, but many pages use inline modal patterns instead of the shared component.
fix: Use the shared src/components/ui/Modal.tsx or src/components/admin/Modal.tsx for all modal dialogs. These already implement aria-modal='true', role='dialog', and close-on-Escape. For custom modals, add a focus trap library (e.g., @radix-ui/react-dialog or focus-trap-react).

title: Contact page has 27 inline color styles - dark mode incompatible
description: The public contact page has 27 inline style={{ color: '#...' }} declarations. These hardcoded colors will not adapt to dark mode, causing potential readability issues (dark text on dark background or vice versa) when users toggle the theme. This is a customer-facing page.
fix: Replace all inline color styles with Tailwind classes that support dark mode variants. Example: style={{ color: '#1e293b' }} becomes className="text-slate-800 dark:text-slate-200".

title: About page has 13 inline color styles - dark mode incompatible
description: The public about page has 13 inline style={{ color: '#...' }} declarations that bypass the dark mode system. Customer-facing page with potential contrast issues in dark mode.
fix: Replace inline colors with Tailwind classes supporting dark mode.

title: Invoices page has 16 inline color styles for invoice rendering
description: The account invoices page has 16 inline style={{ color: '#...' }} declarations. These are likely used for invoice styling/print, but they bypass dark mode support and may cause contrast issues when viewing invoices in dark mode.
fix: For printable content, consider using a print-specific CSS class. For screen display, use Tailwind dark mode classes.

title: Cookies legal page has 11 inline color styles
description: The cookies policy page has 11 inline color styles that bypass the design system and dark mode. Legal pages should be readable in all themes for compliance.
fix: Replace inline colors with Tailwind dark-mode-aware classes.

title: Blog comments component has 16 inline hardcoded colors and 6 in style tags
description: BlogComments.tsx uses 16 inline style color declarations and 6 style={{ color: '#...' }} inline styles. Comments are user-facing content that should respect the user's theme preference. The component appears to render comment content with hardcoded colors that will not adapt to dark mode.
fix: Replace all inline color styles with Tailwind CSS classes that support dark mode. Use text-neutral-600 dark:text-neutral-400 pattern.

title: Root layout properly sets dynamic lang attribute
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The root layout.tsx correctly sets <html lang={locale} dir={dir} data-locale={locale}> where locale is derived from cookies/headers. This ensures proper language declaration for screen readers and search engines. Alternate hreflang links are also generated for all locales.
fix: No action needed. Good implementation.

title: Sample: Payroll page has 17 labels without htmlFor and hardcoded French
description: Representative example of a common pattern: the payroll page has 17 <label> elements, none with htmlFor attributes. Labels are also hardcoded French: 'Annee fiscale', 'Debut de periode', 'Prenom', 'Salaire annuel', 'Taux vacances (%)'. This pattern is repeated across many accounting pages.
fix: Add htmlFor to each label, add id to each input, and replace hardcoded text with t() calls.

title: System/utility admin pages lack i18n
description: Several admin system pages lack i18n: mots-magiques (magic words), medias (old media page), monitoring, system/crons, traductions (translations dashboard). While these are admin-only, they should still support i18n for consistency.
fix: Lower priority. Add i18n when these pages are next modified.

title: Telephonie analytics sub-pages (appels, agents, speech, queues, main) lack i18n
description: All 5 telephonie analytics pages have no i18n. They delegate to client components that also use hardcoded strings.
fix: Add i18n to the client components.

title: Public signup page (tenant registration) lacks i18n - customer-facing
description: The public signup page for new tenant registration uses no i18n hooks. This is a critical conversion page where customers decide to sign up. Non-French speakers cannot understand the form.
fix: Add useI18n() and replace all strings with t() calls. High priority due to conversion impact.

title: All 5 solutions pages lack i18n (cas-usage, partenaires, particuliers, entreprises, main)
description: The solutions section targeting different customer segments (individuals, businesses, partners, use cases) has no i18n. These are marketing pages that should be translated for international audiences.
fix: Add i18n. These are marketing pages - translations should be high quality, not machine-generated.

title: 8 table rows and list items with onClick lack keyboard support
description: Found 8 occurrences of <tr onClick>, <td onClick>, or <li onClick> across 6 files: DataTable.tsx, comptabilite/immobilisations, comptabilite/projets-couts (x2), crm/leads, crm/deals, crm/lists (x2). Clickable table rows need keyboard support for screen reader users navigating in table mode.
fix: For clickable rows, add tabIndex={0}, role='button' (or role='link' if it navigates), and onKeyDown handler. Better pattern: use a <button> or <Link> inside a cell instead of making the entire row clickable.

title: Good overall ARIA adoption with 609 files using aria-* attributes
description: POSITIVE FINDING: 609 files use aria-* attributes, with 340 files using aria-label. Good coverage in: shop components (Header, CartDrawer, SearchModal, ProductCard), form components (AddressAutocomplete, LanguageSelector), voip components (Softphone, IncomingCallModal). Error pages consistently use aria-label for retry buttons.
fix: Continue this good practice. Focus on the gaps identified in other findings (tables without captions, divs without roles).

title: Solutions marketing pages likely missing OG metadata
description: The 5 solutions pages (cas-usage, partenaires, particuliers, entreprises, main) have no generateMetadata() exports. When shared on social media, they will use default/generic OG tags instead of page-specific titles and descriptions. These are marketing pages where social sharing is important.
fix: Add generateMetadata() with page-specific title, description, and og:image for each solutions page.

title: Careers page missing metadata - public HR recruitment page
description: The careers/hiring page has no metadata. This is a public page that should be indexed by search engines and shared on job boards. Missing: title, description, og tags, and potentially JobPosting structured data.
fix: Add generateMetadata() with job-specific SEO. Consider adding JobPosting JSON-LD structured data.

title: 23 public pages missing SEO metadata (contact, help, pricing, about, etc.)
description: 23 customer-facing public pages lack generateMetadata()/metadata exports. Key pages: contact, demo, aide (help), tarifs (pricing), securite, a-propos and sub-pages (valeurs, engagements, equipe, histoire, mission), mentions-legales (3 pages), accessibilite, actualites, clients and sub-pages. These are important for SEO and social sharing.
fix: Add metadata to all public pages. Use generateMetadata() for dynamic locale-aware titles. Include og:image pointing to /api/og route.

title: Accounting batch operations page lacks i18n
description: The batch operations page under accounting has no i18n, using hardcoded French text.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: Email template builder uses escapeAttr/escapeHtml for HTML generation
description: The TemplateBuilder generates email HTML using template strings with escapeAttr() for attributes and escapeHtml() for content. While this is not dangerouslySetInnerHTML in the browser (it generates server-side email HTML), the escapeAttr function should be verified to prevent attribute injection in email templates.
fix: Verify escapeAttr handles all HTML special chars including quotes, angle brackets, and ampersands. Consider using a purpose-built email template library (MJML, react-email) for safer email generation.

file: Multiple admin pages with error silently swallowed
title: Error handling pattern: catch blocks with silent failures
description: Several admin pages follow a pattern of catch {} or catch { // Error handled silently } in fetch calls. Examples: platform/page.tsx:45-46, platform/assisted-setup. When API calls fail, users see no feedback - the page simply doesn't load data or shows stale state. This is poor UX as users don't know if something went wrong.
fix: Add user-visible error feedback in catch blocks: use toast.error() or set an error state that renders an error message. Minimum: toast.error(t('common.error.fetchFailed')).

title: Legal cookie page uses 9 inline color styles from cookies-en.tsx
description: The cookies policy page imports from i18n/legal/cookies-en.tsx which contains 9 inline color styles. These styles are injected into the page content and don't support dark mode. Legal pages have compliance requirements for readability.
fix: Move color definitions from the legal content files to Tailwind CSS classes in the page component. Use dark mode compatible classes.

title: Admin dashboard (most visited admin page) - server page has no i18n but client component does
description: The admin dashboard server component (page.tsx) does not use i18n directly, but correctly delegates to DashboardClient.tsx which DOES use useI18n(). This is the correct pattern - noting it as a reference for how other admin pages should be structured. However, the DashboardClient may still have some hardcoded strings in less visible areas.
fix: No action needed for the server/client split pattern. Audit DashboardClient for remaining hardcoded strings.

title: Legal page content files have inline styles instead of CSS classes
description: The legal page content components (privacy, terms, cookies in both en and fr) use inline style={{ color: '#...' }} for 14+ color values total. These legal content files are separate from the main i18n system (they're full JSX components, not JSON keys) which makes translation management harder.
fix: Consider restructuring legal content to use Tailwind classes for styling and the main i18n JSON system for text content. This would enable easier translation management and dark mode support.

title: Accessibility statement page exists
description: POSITIVE FINDING: An accessibility statement page exists at /accessibilite with proper aria-label. This shows commitment to accessibility compliance.
fix: Ensure the accessibility statement accurately reflects the current state of accessibility compliance and is kept up to date as issues are fixed.

title: Shop header and footer have good ARIA implementation
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The shop Header.tsx uses aria-label extensively (16 occurrences), has proper role attributes, and implements keyboard-accessible navigation. The Footer has aria-label for newsletter input and social links. This is a good reference implementation for other components.
fix: No action needed. Use as reference for fixing other components.

title: Admin forms lack client-side validation feedback patterns
description: Many admin forms use disabled submit buttons (disabled={!field1 || !field2}) but don't explain WHY the button is disabled. Users with cognitive disabilities may not understand which field is missing. The pattern in platform/assisted-setup is: disabled={!client.name || !client.email || !client.slug} with no per-field validation messages.
fix: Add per-field validation with visible error messages. Use aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby pointing to the error message. Show inline errors when fields are empty after the user has interacted with them.

title: Summary: XSS surface area is small and well-managed
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The codebase has only 14 files using dangerouslySetInnerHTML, 12 of which are properly sanitized (DOMPurify or JSON-LD escaping). Only 1 file (tutoriels/page.tsx) needs remediation. The codebase avoids eval() (the one usage in workflow-code-sandbox.ts is properly sandboxed). No new Function() in user-facing code. Overall XSS attack surface is minimal.
fix: Fix the single unsanitized usage in tutoriels/page.tsx (C1-SEC-S-151).

title: Good i18n coverage overall: 248 of 354 pages (70%) use i18n
description: POSITIVE FINDING: 248 pages import and use i18n hooks (useI18n or useTranslation) or server i18n functions. All 22 locale files exist with translations. The i18n system supports client-side (useI18n, useTranslation) and server-side (getServerLocale, createServerTranslator) patterns. Language selector, locale detection, and RTL support (dir attribute) are implemented.
fix: Focus on the remaining 106 pages that lack i18n coverage, prioritizing customer-facing pages.

title: Contact page has no metadata despite being key public page
description: The contact page - one of the most important pages for customer acquisition - has no metadata export. It will appear in search results with generic Next.js title. The page also has 27 inline color styles and 6 form fields that need proper accessibility labels.
fix: Add generateMetadata() with translated title/description. Replace inline styles with Tailwind. Verify all form fields have proper labels.

title: Security audits page lacks i18n
description: The security audits admin page has no i18n integration.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: contentEditable div has proper ARIA attributes
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The email composer's contentEditable div has role='textbox', aria-label='Email body', and aria-multiline='true'. This is a good example of making contentEditable accessible.
fix: No action needed. Use as reference pattern.

title: Shared DataTable component has minimal a11y support
description: The DataTable component (used across many admin pages) has only 3 aria-label occurrences, 2 focus: styles, 1 tabIndex, and 1 clickable <tr>. As the primary data display component in the admin area, it needs better a11y: sortable column headers need aria-sort, clickable rows need keyboard support, pagination needs proper labeling.
fix: Add: (1) aria-sort on sortable column headers, (2) role='button' and keyboard handlers on clickable rows, (3) aria-label on pagination controls, (4) Announce sort changes with aria-live region.

title: Careers page lacks i18n - public customer-facing
description: The careers page is publicly accessible and has no i18n integration. For international recruitment, this page needs to be translatable.
fix: Add i18n with proper translation keys for all career-related content.

title: Public documentation page lacks i18n
description: The public documentation/API docs page has no i18n integration. Documentation should be available in the user's preferred language.
fix: Add i18n support to the documentation page.

title: HeroSlider has good ARIA: 4 aria-labels for controls
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The HeroSlider component has 4 aria-label attributes for navigation controls (previous, next, play/pause, slide indicator). Good reference implementation for carousel accessibility.
fix: Verify aria-label values are translated using t() calls.

title: SearchModal has 4 aria-labels - good search accessibility
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The SearchModal component has 4 aria-label attributes for the search input, results, and modal controls. Proper focus management for keyboard users.
fix: No action needed.

title: Good adoption of Next.js Image component (88 files)
description: POSITIVE FINDING: 88 files import and use the Next.js Image component instead of native . This provides automatic optimization (WebP/AVIF, lazy loading, responsive sizing). The few usages (4 files) are for legitimate cases (data URLs, email HTML generation).
fix: No action needed. Continue using next/image for all standard image rendering.

file: Multiple form pages across admin section
title: Forms use disabled buttons without explaining why action is unavailable
description: A common pattern across admin forms: submit buttons have disabled={!field1 || !field2} conditions. When disabled, the button shows reduced opacity (opacity-50) but no tooltip or message explaining what's missing. This is particularly challenging for users who rely on screen readers, as they encounter a disabled button with no context.
fix: Add aria-disabled instead of disabled, with aria-describedby pointing to helper text that explains what is needed. Or show inline validation messages on empty required fields.

title: Owner dashboard page lacks i18n
description: The owner/dashboard page has no i18n. This is the entry point for business owners.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: VoIP Incoming Call Modal has 5 aria attributes and 3 aria-labels
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The IncomingCallModal has 5 ARIA attributes including aria-live for real-time announcements and proper aria-labels on answer/decline buttons. Good accessibility for an urgent notification component.
fix: No action needed.

title: Skeleton loading component has aria-busy for screen readers
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The Skeleton component uses an ARIA attribute to indicate loading state, allowing screen readers to announce that content is loading.
fix: No action needed.

title: ConfirmDialog has 5 focus: styles - good keyboard focus indication
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The ConfirmDialog component has 5 focus: CSS classes for visible focus indicators. This is important for keyboard users to know which element is focused.
fix: No action needed. Use as reference pattern for other components.

file: 185 files have focus: styles out of 736 with onClick
title: Only 25% of interactive files have visible focus styles
description: 185 files out of 736 files with onClick handlers have focus: CSS classes (25%). This means 75% of interactive components may lack visible focus indicators. WCAG 2.4.7 requires a visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation. While many interactive elements are <button> (which has browser default focus), custom interactive elements and styled buttons with outline-none may lose focus visibility.
fix: Audit interactive components for focus visibility. Add focus:ring-2 focus:ring-offset-2 focus:ring-blue-500 (or equivalent) to all clickable elements. Consider a global CSS rule for focus-visible.

file: admin/abonnement/* (4 pages: plan, domaine, modules, main)
title: Subscription management pages lack i18n
description: All 4 subscription management pages under admin/abonnement have no i18n. These pages let tenants manage their plan, custom domain, and module subscriptions. If tenant admins speak different languages, they cannot use these pages effectively.
fix: Add i18n to all subscription management pages.

title: Sitemap page lacks metadata and i18n
description: The HTML sitemap page has neither metadata nor i18n. While primarily used for SEO, it should have proper metadata and be translatable.
fix: Add metadata and i18n support.

title: Press page lacks i18n - public-facing
description: The press/media page is customer-facing and lacks i18n.
fix: Add i18n support when next modified.

title: RecordingPlayer has 11 aria-labels and 2 tabIndex - good media player a11y
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The call recording player has extensive accessibility support with 11 aria-labels (play, pause, volume, speed, seek), 2 tabIndex for keyboard navigation. Good reference for media player accessibility.
fix: No action needed.

title: Theme toggle has aria-label and focus styles
description: POSITIVE FINDING: Both shop (ui/ThemeToggle.tsx) and admin (admin/ThemeToggle.tsx) theme toggles have aria-label attributes.
fix: Ensure aria-label dynamically reflects the current state: 'Switch to dark mode' vs 'Switch to light mode'.

title: ProductCard has 9 aria-labels - excellent product card accessibility
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The ProductCard component has 9 aria-label attributes covering: add to cart, quick view, wishlist, compare, product link, rating, price, and badge. This is the best-accessibility-annotated component in the codebase.
fix: No action needed. Use as the gold standard reference for other card components.

title: 225 files have <input> elements - need comprehensive label audit
description: 856 <input> elements exist across 225 files. While 168 files have <label> elements (893 total) and 34 files have proper htmlFor linkage, the majority of inputs rely on placeholder text or visual proximity for labeling rather than programmatic association. WCAG 1.3.1 and 4.1.2 require all inputs to have accessible names via label[for], aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Approximately 600+ inputs across ~135 files lack proper accessible names.
fix: Systematic fix: (1) Create a lint rule requiring all <input> to have an associated label. (2) For each form: add htmlFor to labels and matching id to inputs. (3) For inputs without visible labels (search, filter), add aria-label. (4) Automate with eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y.

title: AddressAutocomplete has 14 aria attributes - best practice autocomplete
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The AddressAutocomplete component has 14 aria-* attributes including: aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-activedescendant, aria-autocomplete, role='combobox', role='listbox', role='option'. This follows the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern correctly.
fix: No action needed. Excellent implementation.

title: Translations management page itself lacks i18n (ironic)
description: The admin translations management page (where admins manage translations) does not use i18n for its own UI. The page delegates to TranslationsDashboard.tsx which does use i18n, but the wrapper page itself has no i18n for metadata or page-level strings.
fix: Add i18n to the wrapper page for metadata. Low priority since it delegates to an i18n-aware component.

title: Admin command palette has aria-label and keyboard support
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The AdminCommandPalette (Cmd+K shortcut) has aria-label on the search input and keyboard navigation support. Important for admin power users who rely on keyboard shortcuts.
fix: Ensure results list items have proper roles and are keyboard navigable (role='listbox' and role='option').

title: Ad platform pages lack i18n
description: Three advertising platform pages under admin/media/ lack i18n: ads-youtube, ads-x (Twitter), ads-google.
fix: Add i18n when next modified. Lower priority as these are specialized admin tools.

title: Audit summary statistics - key metrics
description: AUDIT SUMMARY: Total pages scanned: 354 page.tsx files. Components scanned: ~199 component files. Findings: dangerouslySetInnerHTML: 14 files (1 unsafe), eval/new Function: 1 file (sandboxed), i18n coverage: 248/354 (70%), missing i18n: 106 pages, metadata/SEO coverage: 42/354 (12%), loading.tsx coverage: 339/354 (96%), error.tsx coverage: 126 routes, aria-* usage: 609 files, focus: styles: 185 files, labels with htmlFor: 34/168 (20%), tables with caption: 0/130 (0%), native vs next/image: 4 vs 88 files, console.log: 0 actual debug statements, responsive classes: 241/354 pages.
fix: Top priorities: (1) Fix XSS in tutoriels/page.tsx, (2) Add table captions, (3) Add htmlFor to labels, (4) Add i18n to public-facing pages, (5) Add SEO metadata to public pages.

title: 453 outline-none usages suppress default focus indicators
description: Found 453 occurrences of outline-none or outline-0 across 126 files. While 184 files do provide replacement focus styles (focus:ring, focus:outline, etc.), many outline-none usages remove focus visibility without providing an alternative. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible). Concentrated in: checkout/page.tsx (21), ProductEditClient (30), NewProductClient (27), comptabilite/temps (21), media/videos/[id] (13), ivr-builder (11).
fix: Audit each outline-none usage. Either: (1) Remove outline-none if the default browser focus indicator is acceptable, (2) Replace with focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-500 to maintain focus visibility for keyboard users while hiding it for mouse clicks.

file: Only 7 occurrences of focus-visible: across 5 files
title: Almost no use of focus-visible: for keyboard-only focus styles
description: The focus-visible: pseudo-class (which shows focus only for keyboard users, not mouse clicks) is used in only 5 files (7 occurrences): VideoPlayer.tsx, ContentList.tsx, admin/Button.tsx, bannieres/page.tsx, abonnements/page.tsx. The codebase uses 756 focus: occurrences but only 7 focus-visible: ones. Modern best practice is focus-visible: for non-mouse focus indication.
fix: Adopt focus-visible: as the default focus styling approach. Add a Tailwind utility class or custom CSS rule: *:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring-color); outline-offset: 2px; }. Gradually replace focus: with focus-visible: across components.

file: 4 files with arbitrary Tailwind text colors: text-[#xxx]
title: 27 arbitrary Tailwind text color values bypass design system
description: 4 files use text-[#hexcolor] arbitrary Tailwind values (27 occurrences): platform/pricing (7), platform/page (9), platform/demo (3), account/referrals (8). These bypass the design system tokens defined in ios26-tokens.css and may not have proper contrast ratios or dark mode support.
fix: Replace text-[#xxx] with design system Tailwind classes (text-blue-600, text-slate-700, etc.) or CSS custom properties. Verify contrast ratio meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).

title: 49 arbitrary Tailwind background colors bypass design system
description: 27 files use bg-[#hexcolor] arbitrary Tailwind values (49 occurrences). Concentrated in: platform pages (11), account/referrals (9), various shop pages (9), and scattered across other pages. These bypass the design system and may cause visual inconsistency.
fix: Replace with design system Tailwind classes. Use bg-blue-600, bg-slate-100, etc. For brand colors, define them in tailwind.config.ts as custom colors.

title: POSITIVE: Loading states use aria-live for screen reader announcements
description: POSITIVE FINDING: 254 loading.tsx files use aria-live (mostly 'polite') to announce loading states to screen readers. This is excellent accessibility practice - screen reader users are informed when content is loading.
fix: No action needed. Good implementation.

title: Public checkout page for subscription plans lacks i18n
description: The public checkout page for plan subscription (/checkout/[slug]) has no i18n. This is a critical conversion page where customers make purchasing decisions. All pricing, plan description, and form text must be translated.
fix: Add i18n. High priority due to direct revenue impact.

title: Course detail page lacks i18n for page chrome
description: The course detail page under /cours/[slug] does not import any i18n hooks. While course content may come from the database (already translated), the page chrome (buttons, labels, layout elements) is likely hardcoded.
fix: Add i18n for page chrome elements. Course content should come from the database with translated fields.

title: Email HTML body rendered with DOMPurify - properly sanitized
description: POSITIVE: ConversationThread renders email HTML bodies at lines 253 and 279 using DOMPurify.sanitize(item.data.htmlBody). Email bodies from external senders are an XSS risk, but DOMPurify properly neutralizes malicious content.
fix: No action needed. Consider restricting ALLOWED_TAGS further to match email rendering needs.

title: Campaign email preview rendered with DOMPurify - properly sanitized
description: POSITIVE: CampaignEditor renders HTML email preview with DOMPurify.sanitize(htmlContent). Marketing email templates are properly sanitized before preview rendering.
fix: No action needed.

title: Shipping policy page has 3 tables and bg-[#] but no metadata
description: The shipping policy page uses 3 tables to display shipping rates and policies, has an arbitrary background color bg-[#], and lacks metadata/SEO. Policy pages affect customer trust and SEO.
fix: Add metadata, replace arbitrary colors, add table captions.

title: Refund policy page lacks metadata - important for customer trust
description: The refund policy page has no metadata export. This is a trust page that customers check before purchasing. It also has bg-[#] arbitrary color.
fix: Add generateMetadata() with descriptive title and description.

title: CRM duplicates page - one of only 2 files using role='button' correctly
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The CRM duplicates page is one of only 2 files in the codebase that uses role='button' on non-button interactive elements. It also uses tabIndex={0} for keyboard accessibility. Reference implementation.
fix: Propagate this pattern to the other 25 files with <div onClick> and 6 files with <tr>/<li> onClick.

title: Product edit page has 30 outline-none and 30 focus:ring - paired correctly
description: POSITIVE FINDING: ProductEditClient.tsx uses 30 outline-none instances but pairs each with 30 focus:ring replacements. While outline-none suppresses the default browser focus, the focus:ring provides an alternative visible focus indicator. This is the correct pattern.
fix: Consider upgrading to focus-visible:ring-2 for better UX (hides ring on mouse click, shows on keyboard).

title: Referrals page uses 9 bg-[#] and 8 text-[#] arbitrary colors
description: The customer referrals page has extensive arbitrary color usage: 9 bg-[#hexcolor] and 8 text-[#hexcolor] classes. This bypasses the design system and may not support dark mode. This is a customer-facing page where visual consistency matters.
fix: Map all arbitrary colors to the nearest Tailwind color class. Add dark mode variants.

title: Ad platform dashboard pages lack i18n
description: Three ad platform pages (ads-youtube, ads-x, ads-google) lack i18n. These are admin-only but should support multi-language for international teams.
fix: Add i18n when next modified. Lower priority.

title: Order tracking page lacks i18n - customer-facing
description: The public order tracking page (accessible via tracking links in emails) has no i18n. Customers receiving tracking links in their language will see untranslated content.
fix: Add i18n. This page is directly linked from order confirmation emails.

title: API docs page has metadata but uses hardcoded code examples (expected)
description: The API documentation page has proper metadata. Code examples contain console.log() but these are within template literal strings for display purposes, not actual function calls. No i18n concerns as API documentation is typically English-only.
fix: No action needed. Code examples are expected to be in English.

title: Checkout page well-accessibilized with 22 focus:ring, 6 aria-labels, DOMPurify
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The checkout page (highest-value page) has excellent a11y: 22 focus:ring styles for keyboard navigation, 6 aria-label attributes, 25 labels with htmlFor, DOMPurify sanitization for HTML content. It uses i18n via useTranslation. This is the best-implemented page from an a11y perspective.
fix: Use as reference implementation. Consider adding aria-describedby for validation errors on form fields.

title: Signup page well-implemented: 6 htmlFor, 6 focus:ring, 15 aria-*
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The auth signup page is well-implemented with 6 htmlFor on labels, 6 focus:ring styles, 15 aria-* attributes, and full i18n support. Good reference for other auth pages.
fix: No action needed. Good implementation.

title: Peptide calculator has 5 aria-labels for interactive controls
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The PeptideCalculator component has 5 aria-labels for calculation inputs, results, and controls. Good accessibility for a complex interactive widget.
fix: No action needed.

title: Projects/costs page: 36 labels without htmlFor, 3 tables without caption
description: The projects and costs accounting page is one of the worst-offending pages for a11y: 36 <label> elements with zero htmlFor, 3 <table> elements with zero <caption>, 25 <input> elements, 8 <select> elements, and hardcoded French strings throughout.
fix: Comprehensive a11y remediation needed: add htmlFor to all labels, add table captions, add i18n.

title: Public support page lacks i18n
description: The public support page has no i18n. Customer support should be accessible in the user's language.
fix: Add i18n to the support page.

title: UI Modal has 5 aria-* attrs and 2 aria-labels - proper dialog implementation
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The shared Modal component has role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-label/aria-labelledby, and Escape key handling (2 aria-labels total). It also has focus management.
fix: No action needed. Ensure all modal usage in the app uses this component instead of custom implementations.

title: Time tracking page has 21 outline-none, 22 focus:ring, 18 inputs, 4 tables
description: The time tracking accounting page is a complex form with: 18 <input> elements, 6 <select> elements, 4 <table> elements, 18 <label> elements (likely without htmlFor based on pattern), 22 focus:ring (good), but 21 outline-none. Also has 12 hardcoded placeholder strings.
fix: Add htmlFor to labels, add table captions, replace hardcoded placeholders with t() calls.

title: Email admin page has 16 aria-labels, 2 role='button', 7 focus:ring
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The email administration page has excellent a11y with 16 aria-label attributes, 2 role='button' on non-semantic elements, 7 focus:ring styles, and 6 labels. One of the best-accessibilized admin pages.
fix: Use as reference for other admin pages.

title: Client invoices page has 13 aria-labels, 7 focus:ring, 2 tables
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The client invoices accounting page has 13 aria-label attributes, 7 focus:ring styles, and 5 labels with proper associations. However, 2 tables still lack captions.
fix: Add <caption> to the 2 tables.

title: Quick entry page has safe arithmetic parser replacing eval()
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The quick entry page (saisie-rapide) has a comment at line 375 indicating 'SECURITY FIX: Replace eval() with safe arithmetic parser'. The corresponding service file (quick-entry.service.ts:165) confirms 'No eval() or new Function() - fully parsed and computed manually.' This shows proactive security remediation.
fix: No action needed. The safe arithmetic parser approach is correct.

title: Admin Outlook-style layout has 5 aria-labels in folder navigation
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The Outlook-style folder pane has 5 ARIA attributes for navigation tree items. The ContentList has aria-live for dynamic updates and 2 focus-visible: styles.
fix: Verify tree navigation follows WAI-ARIA treeview pattern (role='tree', role='treeitem', aria-expanded).

title: Custom reports page has 14 labels, 8 selects, 7 focus:ring but large and complex
description: The custom reports builder is a complex admin page with 14 labels, 11 inputs, 8 select elements, 7 focus:ring styles, and 2 tables. It has metadata (good) and i18n (good). One of the better-implemented accounting pages, but labels likely lack htmlFor.
fix: Add htmlFor to all 14 labels. Add captions to 2 tables.

title: Privacy and Terms pages use separate JSX components instead of i18n JSON
description: The privacy and terms pages import from i18n/legal/ which are full JSX components (privacy-fr.tsx, privacy-en.tsx, terms-fr.tsx, terms-en.tsx). This approach only supports 2 languages (en/fr) while the app supports 22. Other languages will see the English version by default. These are legal compliance pages.
fix: Consider using the main i18n JSON system for legal pages, with professional translations for all 22 locales. Legal content must be accurately translated for compliance.

title: Platform pricing page uses 7 text-[#] and 4 bg-[#] arbitrary colors
description: The platform pricing page (public-facing, conversion-critical) uses 7 arbitrary text colors and 4 arbitrary background colors. Also has 2 tables without captions and metadata (good). The arbitrary colors may not meet contrast requirements.
fix: Replace all arbitrary colors with design system colors. Verify contrast ratios.

title: Video management page: 25 aria-labels, 20 inputs - good a11y
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The video management admin page has 25 aria-label attributes (highest in admin), 17 placeholder={t()} calls, 20 inputs, and 12 selects. Good reference for media management accessibility.
fix: No critical action needed.

title: Cookie consent banner has aria-label
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The cookie consent component has aria-label for the consent banner and accept button. Important for compliance.
fix: No action needed.

title: Checkout success page lacks metadata
description: The checkout success/thank you page has no metadata. While this page is behind authentication, it could benefit from proper titles for browser tab identification and share functionality.
fix: Add metadata with a translated thank you title.

title: Text-to-speech button has 2 aria-labels
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The TextToSpeechButton component has 2 aria-labels for the button states (play/stop). Good accessibility for vision-impaired users who might want product descriptions read aloud.
fix: No action needed.

title: Shop CartDrawer has 11 aria-labels, focus-ring, and live region
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The shop CartDrawer component has 11 aria-label attributes, 1 aria-live region for cart updates, 1 focus:ring style, 8 ARIA attributes total from cart/CartDrawer. One of the best-accessibilized shop components. Properly announces cart changes to screen readers.
fix: No action needed. Use as reference.

title: Monitoring page lacks i18n
description: The admin monitoring page has no i18n integration. System monitoring dashboards should be translatable for international admin teams.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: MegaMenu has aria-label and next/image
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The MegaMenu navigation component uses aria-label for menu sections and next/image for optimized images.
fix: Verify keyboard navigation works (arrow keys for menu items, Escape to close).

title: Media library has 21 aria-labels, 3 div onClick, needs keyboard fix
description: The media library page has 21 aria-label attributes (excellent) but also 3 <div onClick> handlers for media item selection. The clickable divs need role='button' and keyboard support for accessibility-compliant media selection.
fix: Add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown to the 3 clickable divs. Or replace with <button> elements.

title: Admin dashboard server wrapper correctly delegates i18n to client component
description: POSITIVE PATTERN: The admin dashboard page.tsx is a server component that fetches data and passes it to DashboardClient.tsx which handles i18n. This is the recommended pattern for Next.js App Router - server-side data, client-side i18n.
fix: Propagate this pattern to other admin pages that are currently large client components doing their own data fetching.

title: Product carousel has 2 aria-labels for navigation
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The ProductCarousel has aria-labels for previous/next navigation buttons.
fix: Add aria-roledescription='carousel' and aria-label describing the carousel content.

title: PWA install prompt has aria-label
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The PWA install prompt button has an aria-label attribute.
fix: No action needed.

title: Back-to-top button has aria-label
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The back-to-top floating button has an aria-label for screen readers.
fix: No action needed.

title: Blog post content rendered with comprehensive DOMPurify config
description: POSITIVE FINDING: Blog post content is rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML with a comprehensive DOMPurify configuration that explicitly lists 21 allowed tags (p, br, strong, em, ul, ol, li, h2-h6, a, span, blockquote, img, figure, figcaption, table, thead, tbody, tr, th, td, pre, code, div, sub, sup, hr) and 9 allowed attributes (href, target, rel, class, src, alt, width, height, style). This is well-balanced - permissive enough for rich content but restrictive enough to prevent XSS.
fix: No action needed. Consider removing 'style' from ALLOWED_ATTR if CSS injection is a concern (attackers can exfiltrate data via style attributes).

title: Media images page: 13 aria-labels, 4 div onClick needs keyboard
description: The media images management page has 13 aria-label attributes but 4 <div onClick> handlers for image selection/actions. These clickable divs need keyboard support.
fix: Add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler to the 4 clickable divs.

title: Telephonie settings: 9 aria-labels, 10 inputs, 3 focus:ring
description: The telephonie settings page has good aria-label coverage (9) but also has 10 inputs, 3 hardcoded placeholder strings, and 2 selects. It uses focus:ring (3) but also outline-none (3).
fix: Add i18n for hardcoded strings. Verify labels are linked to inputs.

title: CRM forecast page lacks i18n
description: The CRM sales forecast page has no i18n integration. This is a high-value analytics page for sales managers.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: Orders page: 11 labels, 14 focus:ring, 1 table, 1 aria-label
description: The customer orders page has 11 labels with proper htmlFor (good), 14 focus:ring styles, 1 table (needs caption), and 1 aria-label. Well-implemented with i18n.
fix: Add <caption> to the orders table.

title: CompareBar has 3 aria-labels and aria-live for comparison updates
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The product comparison bar has 3 aria-labels for clear, compare, and remove buttons, plus aria-live for announcing comparison changes.
fix: No action needed.

title: CRM scheduling page: 14 labels, 8 selects, 6 inputs, 4 focus:ring, 3 tables
description: The CRM scheduling page is a complex form-heavy page with 14 labels, 8 select elements, 6 inputs, 4 focus:ring styles, and 3 tables. Has i18n support. One of the more feature-rich admin pages.
fix: Add captions to 3 tables. Verify all 14 labels have htmlFor.

title: Course description rendered with DOMPurify - restricted tags
description: POSITIVE FINDING: Course product descriptions use DOMPurify.sanitize with a restricted set of tags: p, br, strong, em, ul, ol, li, h2-h4, a, span, blockquote. Attributes limited to href, target, rel, class. Secure implementation.
fix: No action needed.

title: Security page lacks metadata and has bg-[#] color
description: The public security/trust page has no metadata and uses arbitrary background colors. This is an important trust page for potential customers evaluating security practices.
fix: Add metadata with security-focused title/description. Replace arbitrary colors.

title: Help page lacks metadata
description: The public help/support page has no metadata. Help pages are frequently searched by existing customers.
fix: Add metadata with translated help-related title and description.

title: Pricing page lacks metadata - critical conversion page
description: The public pricing page has no metadata. This is one of the most important pages for conversion - potential customers searching for pricing will see generic browser titles.

title: News/updates page lacks metadata
description: The public news/updates page has no metadata. News pages benefit from SEO metadata for indexing.
fix: Add metadata.

title: Tenant detail page lacks i18n
description: The super-admin tenant detail page has no i18n integration. Used for managing individual tenants.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: Financial statements page has 4 tables - needs captions
description: The financial statements page has 4 tables for balance sheet, income statement, etc. None have captions. Financial tables are critical for screen reader users who need to distinguish between different statement types.
fix: Add descriptive <caption> to each table: 'Balance Sheet', 'Income Statement', etc. using t() for translations.

title: Forecast dashboard has 4 tables, 2 selects, 2 focus-ring
description: The CRM forecast dashboard renders 4 tables for forecast data. None have captions, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand which forecast metric each table represents.
fix: Add descriptive captions to all 4 tables.

title: Checkout page DOMPurify config limits tags to <a> and <span> only
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The checkout page sanitizes HTML with a very restrictive DOMPurify config: ALLOWED_TAGS limited to just 'a' and 'span', ALLOWED_ATTR limited to 'href', 'target', 'rel', 'class'. This is the most restrictive sanitization in the codebase - appropriate for the highest-value page.

title: About/values sub-page lacks metadata

title: All 6 about section pages lack metadata
description: Six pages under /a-propos/ lack metadata: main, valeurs, engagements, equipe, histoire, mission. These are brand pages important for SEO and social sharing.

title: Legal pages (confidentialite, conditions, cookies) lack metadata
description: Three legal pages under /mentions-legales/ lack metadata: confidentialite (privacy in French), conditions, cookies. These have hardcoded inline styles and should have SEO metadata for compliance page discoverability.
fix: Add metadata. Legal pages should be easily findable via search.

title: Accessibility statement page lacks metadata
description: The accessibility statement page has no metadata despite being important for compliance. It has an aria-label attribute (good) and i18n support.

title: Client showcase pages lack metadata (references, etudes-de-cas, temoignages)
description: Four client-facing pages have no metadata: clients (main), references, etudes-de-cas (case studies), temoignages (testimonials). These are important social proof pages for potential customers.

title: Magic words admin page lacks i18n
description: The admin magic words configuration page has no i18n integration.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: Demo page lacks metadata despite being conversion-critical
description: The public demo request page has no metadata. This is a key conversion page where potential customers request demonstrations.
